digest.component_or_area: identity entity + authorship certification + Jira user-mapping reconciler
propositions: rebar attributes operations only via an unauthenticated git-config author string, so events cannot be reliably attributed to a real, non-repudiable person.

Introduce a first-class identity entity storing name, email, {provider,external_id} mappings and public keys only, with a write-time guard rejecting private-key material; private keys stay in ssh-agent/~/.ssh.

Authorship signatures use a DSSE envelope wrapping an in-toto Statement whose subject binds {event_uuid, content_hash}, verified offline against the identity's in-band keys with unsigned Tier-0 fallback.
Key validity uses TOFU genesis + G2 (each key change signed by a currently-valid key) with era scoped by tickets-branch commit ancestry refined by intra_idx, not HLC epoch.

Provider-neutral mapping keys on the provider's opaque id (Jira accountId), resolving outbound assignee/reporter with best-effort bootstrap and graceful degradation, and auto-upgrading placeholders for unmapped inbound users idempotently.

Enforcement is opt-in via a CI merge-gate running verify-identity over the merged log, failing non-verified events since a cutover ref while replay never rejects and default install is byte-identical to pre-feature.
